title: Indonesia's Coal Dominance in China is Waning
section_path: Indonesia's Coal Dominance in China is Waning
section_path_text: Indonesia's Coal Dominance in China is Waning
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
Indonesia has remained China's primary source of coal imports, and Russia its second largest source of coal imports, and of note is that Australia has finally come close to overtaking Mongolia as China's third largest source. The most recently released data shows that China's coal imports from Indonesia totaled 19.2 million tons in July, which is up month-on-month by 2.6 million tons (16%) and up year-on-year by 3.4 million tons (22%). Image reference: Chart 1 (1).jpg | assets/2024-09-09_indonesias-coal-dominance-in-china-is-waning_img_chart128129_41786037f1cc.jpg
Overall, Indonesia remains China's primary source of coal imports, but its dominance has been waning as we have been discussing in Commodore Research's Weekly Dry Bulk Reports.
